prompt: Write a 150-word short story about a lighthouse keeper who discovers something unexpected in the fog. Do not use dialogue.
response: The lighthouse keeper stood at the peak of the rocky outcropping, scanning the fog-shrouded horizon. As a stalwart guardian of the coast, he knew every contour, every wave pattern. But tonight was different. As he gazed out into the whirling mist, a faint sparkle caught his eye. A figure, indistinct at first, began to take shape. He squinted, straining through the veil of fog. A boat, weathered and small, drifted aimlessly toward the shore. Confusion furrowed his brow – boats didn't venture into this treacherous stretch of coast. A wise, old habit whispered him to be cautious, yet his curiosity got the better of him. As the vessel drew closer, he caught a glimpse of a bundle lashed to the deck. His heart quickened. Something was off. Suddenly, the fog swirled more turbulent, as if sensing the keeper's unease. He stood frozen, eyes fixed upon the ship, his mind racing with the implications.
